title: Generate flowchart for process
prompt: Create a Mermaid diagram showing the order fulfillment process from cart to delivery
expected_behavior: Produces a syntactically valid flowchart (flowchart TD or LR) with descriptive node IDs, labeled edges, subgraphs for logical groupings, max 20 nodes, and an accessibility text summary before the diagram.

title: Generate C4 architecture diagram
prompt: Visualize the system architecture for our microservices platform as a C4 context diagram
expected_behavior: Produces a valid C4Context Mermaid diagram with Person, System, and System_Ext nodes, labeled relationships, and appropriate boundary groupings.

title: Content requires more than 20 nodes
prompt: Diagram all 30 services in our platform and their dependencies
expected_behavior: Splits into 2+ diagrams with cross-references rather than cramming 30 nodes into one. Each sub-diagram has max 20 nodes with clear indication of how they connect.

title: No diagram type specified
prompt: Visualize how the authentication flow works between the mobile app, API gateway, and identity provider
expected_behavior: Auto-selects sequenceDiagram as the optimal type for actor interactions over time. Includes participant declarations, labeled messages, and alt/opt blocks for conditional flows.

title: False positive — data better as table
prompt: Show me a comparison of the response times for each of our 5 API endpoints
expected_behavior: Does NOT generate a diagram. Recognizes that metric comparisons are better represented as a table, and produces a markdown table instead.
